applications.name: MK_editor
applications.purpose: Desktop editor shell with docking UI, project creation wizard, project bundle save/open commands, command-driven View and Run menus, searchable Command Palette, Edit undo/redo menu, SceneAuthoringDocument-backed hierarchy rows, selected-node state, create/rename/delete/duplicate actions, selected-subtree prefab save/load/instantiate controls, scene reference diagnostics, scene package candidate rows, package registration draft rows, Apply Package Registration button for reviewed add_runtime_file rows, Inspector transform and camera/light/mesh/sprite component editing through scene-authoring actions, viewport transform delta controls through scene-authoring actions, dirty status, project document state, filterable asset browser, asset import queue, selected cooked material preview status/artifact/diagnostic/uniform/base-color/emissive/texture-dependency display plus GPU binding preview diagnostics, Import Assets action wired through RootedFileSystem and execute_asset_import_plan for first-party texture/mesh/audio/material artifacts with successful imports reflected in the Content Browser, hot reload event, recook request, applied result, and failed rollback diagnostic display, editor recook execution through build_asset_recook_plan plus execute_asset_import_plan, shader compile status display, shader tool discovery from project-relative roots plus MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, vcpkg tool roots, and known installed Windows SDK/Vulkan SDK locations with D3D12/Vulkan/Metal toolchain readiness diagnostics and explicit DXC SPIR-V CodeGen status, D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V viewport shader artifact readiness display, editable viewport render backend and shader tool Project Settings, project-setting-driven Compile Shader action that queues D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V viewport shader requests through RootedFileSystem, Win32ProcessRunner, ShaderToolProcessRunner, and execute_shader_compile_action, conditional D3D12 IRhiDevice viewport bridge activation after MK_editor_core verifies non-empty VS/PS DXIL artifacts exist, RHI-backed scene viewport submission through build_scene_render_packet and MK_scene_renderer with mesh and sprite draw stats, selected-material GPU binding preview rendering through MK_runtime_rhi texture uploads, material descriptor binding, a small RhiViewportSurface, and D3D12 shared texture or CPU readback SDL display, D3D12 shared texture wrapping through the SDL3 D3D12 renderer when available, CPU readback fallback into a GUI-local SDL viewport display texture shown through Dear ImGui, viewport display path diagnostics, viewport toolbar, Play/Pause/Stop controls, timeline playback controls and event table adapted from MK_editor_ui models, recorder-backed Profiler panel that records editor.frame plus editor counters through MK_core DiagnosticsRecorder and renders MK_editor_core summary/profile/counter/event/status/trace-export/trace-file-save/trace-save-dialog/telemetry-handoff/trace-import-review/trace-file-import/trace-open-dialog rows with first-party exported Trace Event JSON capture reconstruction counts, owns SDL-backed Browse Save Trace JSON and Browse Trace JSON native dialogs for in-project .json trace save/import review, viewport state display, and Scene, Inspector, Assets, Console, Viewport, Timeline, Profiler, and Project Settings panels. GPU markers, allocator diagnostics, backend timestamps, telemetry SDK/upload execution, crash reports, broader editor native save/open dialogs outside Profiler, arbitrary trace conversion beyond first-party exported Trace Event JSON reconstruction, and production flame graphs remain future observability work. Dear ImGui is scoped to developer/editor tooling and is not the production runtime UI foundation.
